calendar.matters:
Petitioner Michael Ramirez Motion On Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus

Off Calendar Jul-11-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. LINA BALCIUNAS COCKRELL, Esq. of Messing Adam & Jasmine LLP (Tel: 415-266-1800) for petitioner Michael Ramirez;
Atty. GRAHAM C. MILLS, Esq. of California Attorney General's Office (Tel: 510-879-0790) for Real Party in Interest California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection (via Zoom videoconference).

Hearing Re: Petitioner Michael Ramirez's Motion On Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Ruling On Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Mar-21-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER MICHAEL RAMIREZ Notice Of Motion And Motion For Ruling On Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus

Continued to be heard on April 12, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. before Judge Harold Kahn in department 302.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com mailto:maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Motion For Leave To Amend Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Mar-21-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER MICHAEL RAMIREZ Motion For Leave To Amend Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus

Grant. No opposition filed. Petitioner shall immediately file the amended petition with the new verification as the court does not deem pleadings filed. Even though the amended petition supersedes the original petition on which the companion motion for writ is based, the court will address the merits of the writ of administrative mandamus as the amendment does not affect the substance of the companion motion. As noted below, that hearing will occur before Judge Kahn on April 12, 2022.

Counsel for the prevailing party is submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com mailto:maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
